message:
fix(telemetry/latency): compute true median on even-length samples

calculate_latency_stats() always returned `sorted[len/2]` as the
median, which is the *upper* middle element for even-length inputs
and therefore biased. For the canonical n=2 case (50ms, 150ms) the
reported median was 150 instead of 100, propagating a 50% upward
bias into every dashboard that consumes latency medians.

Average the two middle elements for even n; keep the single-element
pick for odd n. The partial_cmp fallback already protected the sort
from NaN panics (called out in a new comment).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
